title: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

seo.imageAlt: Perfectly cooked New York Strip Steak with melted garlic butter and fresh herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in a restaurant-quality New York Strip Steak, infused with rich garlic butter, right in your own kitchen.

description: Indulge in a restaurant-quality New York Strip Steak, infused with rich garlic butter, right in your own kitchen. This American classic delivers incredible flavor and a tender bite, perfect for a special dinner. Prepare to impress with this simple yet sophisticated steak recipe.

ingredients.notes: or high smoke point oil like grapeseed or avocado

instructions.title: Prepare and Season Steaks
instructions.text: Remove steaks from the refrigerator at least 30 minutes before cooking to bring them to room temperature. Pat them very dry with paper towels. Season generously on all sides with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper.

instructions.title: Preheat Skillet
instructions.text: Heat a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat until it just begins to smoke. Add the olive oil to the hot pan and swirl to coat the bottom.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks in the hot skillet. Sear for 2-4 minutes per side for a medium-rare steak, or longer for desired doneness, until a deep brown crust forms. Do not overcrowd the pan; cook in batches if necessary.

instructions.title: Add Garlic Butter and Herbs
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Add the cold butter cubes, smashed garlic cloves, rosemary, and thyme sprigs to the skillet. As the butter melts, tilt the pan and spoon the melted garlic herb butter over the steaks continuously for 1-2 minutes.

instructions.title: Finish Cooking and Rest
instructions.text: Continue basting the steaks, flipping once, until they reach your desired internal temperature. For medium-rare, aim for 130-135°F (54-57°C). Remove the steaks from the skillet and transfer them to a cutting board. Tent loosely with foil.

instructions.title: Serve with Pan Juices
instructions.text: Let the steaks rest for at least 10 minutes before slicing against the grain. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and flavorful result. Drizzle with the remaining garlic butter from the pan and serve immediately.

tags: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter
tips: Always pat your steaks thoroughly dry before searing. Excess moisture creates steam, which prevents a good crust from forming.
For the best sear, use a cast-iron skillet. It retains heat exceptionally well, ensuring an even and beautiful brown crust on your steak.
Resting your steak after cooking is crucial. It allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, resulting in a more tender and flavorful bite. Don't skip this step!
article: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is a beloved American Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
